## Teacher guidance

A good measurement plan is simple, consistent, and tied to the goal of the
project.

### Characteristics of a strong submission

- It is clear which task is being compared before and after.
- There are few enough measurement points that the participant will actually
  keep the log.
- The logbook distinguishes between observations and assumptions.
- The plan explains how quality will be assessed, not just time spent.
- The risk assessment is connected to the specific work process.

### Common weaknesses

- Too many measurement points without realistic data collection.
- Comparison of different kinds of tasks that are not directly comparable.
- No plan for quality assurance of the output.
- No reflection on data, privacy, or internal policy.
